meta.title: Colorado LIHTC Equity Pricing Factor Snapshot
meta.methodology: Curated Colorado-specific factors that can move LIHTC equity pricing away from the national Novogradac benchmark. This file documents verified public inputs and qualitative pressure signals only; it does not model cents-per-credit pricing.
meta.important_disclaimer: Do not treat these factors as a pricing formula. Use data/market/novogradac-equity-pricing.json as the market-price anchor, then use this file to disclose Colorado-specific factors that require deal counsel, syndicator, and CHFA verification.

state_affordable_housing_tax_credit.label: Colorado State Affordable Housing Tax Credit
state_affordable_housing_tax_credit.pricing_relevance: State AHTC can add a second tax-credit equity stream to Colorado LIHTC transactions, especially federal 9% or 4% deals paired with state credit. Investor pricing must be verified deal-by-deal.
state_affordable_housing_tax_credit.claiming_schedule: HB24-1434 accelerated the Colorado AHTC so qualified taxpayers claim 70% in year one and 6% in each of years two through six.
state_affordable_housing_tax_credit.allocation_cadence: CHFA allocates state AHTC through its Housing Tax Credit program, with annual reports and housing-credit rounds documenting awards.

state_credit_pricing.source_note: No fetchable official CHFA or state source reviewed on 2026-07-17 published current cents-per-credit pricing for Colorado state AHTC. Keep pricing null until an official or directly citable market source publishes it.

property_insurance_regional_adjustment.label: Property-insurance shock: hail and wildfire
property_insurance_regional_adjustment.pricing_relevance: Higher property-insurance expectations can pressure underwriting NOI and investor yield in Colorado hail, wildfire, and mountain/WUI submarkets. This is a regional diligence factor, not a benchmark-price formula.
property_insurance_regional_adjustment.source_note: Colorado DOI 2026 carrier-data release identifies hail as a large homeowner-insurance premium driver and wildfire as a targeted high-risk factor; COHO local climate-hazard data identifies hail and wildfire risk geographies.

pab_volume_cap_pressure.label: PAB volume-cap pressure
pab_volume_cap_pressure.formula: data/policy/pab-allocations.json metadata ratios only; no demand/application oversubscription ratio is computed without a verified demand source.
pab_volume_cap_pressure.pricing_relevance: Federal 4% LIHTC execution depends on finite Colorado private-activity-bond volume cap. Limited cap and the 25% bond-financing test expansion are qualitative 4% supply/closing-timing pressure signals.
pab_volume_cap_pressure.source_note: Existing DOLA allocation artifact provides cap distribution, not verified demand/applications. Treat this as a pressure indicator rather than proof of oversubscription.

local_government_rofr_rofo.label: Colorado local-government ROFR/ROFO
local_government_rofr_rofo.pricing_relevance: Colorado local-government ROFR/ROFO can affect exit timing and preservation-transfer diligence for existing affordable multifamily assets.
local_government_rofr_rofo.source_note: HB23-1190 was vetoed and did not become law; the enacted Colorado ROFR/ROFO authority is HB24-1175.
